conducted — showing INEGI capacity for large statistical operations. WB SCI
approximately 78. INEGI maintaining strong statistical infrastructure despite
fiscal austerity pressures affecting public sector generally.

sources.citation: World Bank Statistical Capacity Indicator, Mexico 2015; INEGI Encuesta Intercensal 2015

2016.assessment:
Statistical capacity broadly stable at good level. Some concerns about resource
constraints due to fiscal austerity. Mexico's crime victimization surveys
(ENVIPE) showing much higher crime than official reports — good sign of
statistical independence even when inconvenient for government. WB SCI approximately 78.

sources.citation: World Bank Statistical Capacity Indicator, Mexico 2016

2017.assessment:
Good statistical capacity maintained. INEGI producing full range of statistics
including crime victimization (ENVIPE), economic censuses, national accounts,
price indices. Mexico among better statistical systems in Latin America. Some
improvement in real-time data publication.

sources.citation: World Bank Statistical Capacity Indicator, Mexico 2017

2018.assessment:
Near peak statistical capacity for this period. 2020 census preparation underway.
INEGI continuing autonomy. Data portals well-developed. AMLO won election July 2018;
his government would later face criticism for dismissing statistical measures
(poverty data) that contradicted government claims. WB SCI approximately 79.

sources.citation: World Bank Statistical Capacity Indicator, Mexico 2018

2019.assessment:
AMLO government questioning CONEVAL (social evaluation agency) poverty statistics
and INEGI data that showed economic contraction — raising concerns about statistical
independence. INEGI responded by defending its methodology publicly. WB SCI
approximately 79. Statistical infrastructure maintained but concerns about political
pressure on statistical agencies.

sources.citation: World Bank Statistical Capacity Indicator, Mexico 2019; INEGI-AMLO tensions

2020.assessment:
WB SCI last year: 2020 approximately 79 for Mexico. COVID complicated census
operations — 2020 population census conducted but with difficulties. AMLO government
cut INEGI budget significantly in 2019-2020 austerity measures. INEGI chief
statistician complained about budget cuts threatening statistical capacity.
WB replaced SCI with Statistical Performance Indicators (SPI) from 2021.

sources.citation: World Bank Statistical Capacity Indicator, Mexico 2020 (final measurement)

2021.assessment:
SCI discontinued; WB SPI (Statistical Performance Indicators) replaced it. Mexico's
SPI score for 2021 approximately 77-80 equivalent. INEGI budget cuts under AMLO
austerity affecting capacity. 2020 census published with some delays. CONEVAL
poverty measurement continuing independently despite government pressure. Some
statistical capacity decline from budget cuts.

sources.citation: World Bank SPI 2021 (Mexico); INEGI annual report 2021

2022.assessment:
Statistical capacity declining slightly under AMLO austerity. INEGI budget
cuts affecting some survey programs. AMLO government continuing to challenge
official poverty statistics (CONEVAL). INEGI maintaining core statistical
outputs but with reduced resources. SPI equivalent approximately 76.

sources.citation: World Bank SPI 2022; INEGI under austerity

2023.assessment:
Statistical capacity broadly maintained at good level despite resource constraints.
INEGI autonomous and producing regular statistical outputs. Economic census
preparation. Crime victimization survey (ENVIPE) continuing. Mexico maintains
adequate statistical transparency by regional standards. SPI equivalent approximately 76.

sources.citation: World Bank SPI 2023; INEGI annual report

2024.assessment:
Statistical capacity likely broadly maintained under Sheinbaum government.
Sheinbaum more orthodox on institutional matters than AMLO. INEGI continuing
autonomous operations. Some hope for budget restoration. 2025 census planning.
WB SPI 2024 not yet published; estimated at approximately 2023 level.

sources.citation: Estimated from trend; WB SPI 2024 not yet published

2025.assessment:
Statistical capacity data for 2025 not available. INEGI expected to continue
autonomous operations. Sheinbaum government expected to be less hostile to
statistical agencies than AMLO. No major changes expected in statistical
transparency. WB SPI 2025 not yet published.

sources.citation: INEGI institutional status; no 2025 data available
